question: Describe the purpose of the SQLiteOpenHelper class in Android and explain the roles of its two key callback methods.
expectedPoints: SQLiteOpenHelper is a helper class to manage database creation and version management.
The onCreate(SQLiteDatabase db) method is called when the database is created for the first time. It is used to execute the initial CREATE TABLE statements.

The onUpgrade(SQLiteDatabase db, int oldVersion, int newVersion) method is called when the database needs to be upgraded to a new version. It is used to alter the database schema, e.g., by dropping old tables and creating new ones.

question: Explain why transactions are important when performing multiple database operations in SQLite and describe the basic methods used to control one.
expectedPoints: Transactions group multiple operations into a single atomic unit of work. They ensure that either all operations succeed (are committed) or none of them do (are rolled back), maintaining database integrity.
beginTransaction() is used to start a new transaction.
setTransactionSuccessful() is called to mark the current transaction as successful. Without this call, all operations will be rolled back.
endTransaction() ends the transaction. If setTransactionSuccessful() was called, it commits the changes; otherwise, it rolls them back.

question: What is the fundamental first step an Android application must take to begin interacting with an SQLite database?
expectedPoints: The application must create an instance of, or get a reference to, an SQLiteOpenHelper class.

Calling the getWritableDatabase() or getReadableDatabase() method on the SQLiteOpenHelper instance is the step that actually triggers the database to be opened or created, providing a SQLiteDatabase object for interaction.
